overriding-and-specialization.label: Overriding and Specialization
overriding-and-specialization.summary: Override a method when a subclass needs the same message name but a different implementation.

ci-equipment-override-practice.title: Specialize equipment checkout labels
ci-equipment-override-practice.prompt: Complete `Laptop.checkout_label()` so it overrides the base equipment label.
ci-equipment-override-practice.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

ci-equipment-override-practice.starterCode:
from models.laptop import Laptop

laptop = Laptop("MacBook", "M-22")
print(laptop.checkout_label())

sc-overriding-and-specialization-design.title: Check the design choice
sc-overriding-and-specialization-design.prompt: Which choice best matches the lesson goal for overriding and specialization?
sc-overriding-and-specialization-design.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Override a method when a subclass needs the same message name but a different implementation.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.a: Put every class in main.py so imports are never needed.
options.b: Give each class or service a focused responsibility and import it where needed.
options.c: Avoid methods so every object exposes all of its data directly.
options.d: Copy the same logic into each subclass to avoid shared code.

try-overriding-and-specialization-sketch.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.

try-overriding-and-specialization-sketch.title: Use super and specialize a label
try-overriding-and-specialization-sketch.prompt: Complete `BugTicket.label()` by reusing base initialization and returning a specialized label.
try-overriding-and-specialization-sketch.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.

concept.title: Overriding and Specialization: the idea
concept.bodyMarkdown: **Overriding and Specialization** is not just syntax. In this course it is practiced inside small projects where one file owns one clear part of the program. Override a method when a subclass needs the same message name but a different implementation.

multifile-shape.title: Overriding and Specialization: the file shape
multifile-shape.bodyMarkdown: The file layout matters. Models hold object state and behavior, services coordinate groups of objects, tests verify behavior, and `main.py` stays thin enough to read.

graded-habit.title: Overriding and Specialization: what the grader checks
graded-habit.bodyMarkdown: The grader checks behavior from the running project. A strong answer keeps imports working, keeps all files present, and proves the object behavior through output or assertions.
